description: Holding Compose UI data in a plain variable instead of observable State, so the screen never recomposes when it changes.
body: Failure mode: a composable stores changing data in a regular var rather than mutableStateOf or a collected StateFlow, so the runtime has nothing to observe and never recomposes; the UI shows stale data with no error. Detection signals: data changes but the UI doesn't update; adding an unrelated recomposition trigger 'fixes' it; the value isn't Compose State. Do not back UI on non-observable variables. Safer fallback: store transient state in remember { mutableStateOf(...) }, collect ViewModel state with collectAsStateWithLifecycle, hoist state to its proper owner, and confirm the UI updates from the state change alone.
